description:
Production observability dashboard for dlq-service. Covers DLQ ingestion, Kafka lag, Mongo persistence latency/errors, HTTP query API health, and JVM/process capacity.

How to use this dashboard: Start from the top Overview row to verify SLO health, then drill into throughput, latency, errors, and capacity sections to isolate bottlenecks. Use the template filters (environment, instance, topic, job type, endpoint, and command where available) to narrow scope during incidents and compare behavior across dimensions. Panel info icon meaning: Click the i icon in each panel header to see what the metric represents, how to interpret normal vs abnormal values, and what operational signal it provides.

panels.description: Total jobs persisted to DLQ storage over selected time range.
panels.title: Jobs Persisted (range)

panels.description: Current DLQ persistence rate.
panels.title: DLQ Persist Rate

panels.description: Kafka records consumed by dlq-service per second.
panels.title: Kafka Consumed Rate

panels.description: Maximum Kafka consumer lag for dlq-service.
panels.title: Max Lag

panels.description: Share of Mongo commands that fail.
panels.title: Mongo Failed %

panels.description: Target availability for dlq-service.
panels.title: Availability
DLQ Ingestion Flow
panels.description: Compares Kafka consumed rate with persisted-to-storage rate for DLQ traffic. Use this to verify ingestion-to-persistence continuity and detect when writes fall behind consumption.

panels.title: Persist and Consume Rate by Dimension
panels.description: Breaks down persist and consume throughput by job type and topic. Helps identify skewed workload patterns, dominant failure classes, and dimension-specific traffic anomalies.

panels.title: Lag by Topic (Current and Max)
panels.description: Tracks current and maximum lag per topic for DLQ consumers. Current lag reflects live backlog; max lag shows worst-case pressure. Sustained growth indicates consumer capacity shortfall.

panels.title: Consumer Efficiency
panels.description: Shows consumer byte rate plus fetch efficiency indicators (records per request and fetch size). Use this to tune fetch behavior and identify inefficient polling patterns that impact lag and latency.

panels.title: dlq-service — HTTP Latency p50/p90/p95/p99

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))
histogram_quantile(0.90, sum by (le) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))
histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))
histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))

panels.description: p50 / p90 / p95 / p99 HTTP latency for dlq-service (all endpoints combined).

panels.title: dlq-service — p50/p90/p99 per Endpoint

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le, uri) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))
histogram_quantile(0.90, sum by (le, uri) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))
histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, uri) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))

panels.description: Per-endpoint p50 / p90 / p99 HTTP latency for dlq-service.

panels.title: Kafka Listener Latency (p50/p90/p95/p99)
panels.description: Displays DLQ listener processing latency percentiles. p50 reflects typical performance, while p95/p99 capture tail delays and outliers critical for SLO and incident analysis.

panels.title: Listener Throughput and Mean Latency
panels.description: Correlates listener throughput with average processing latency to reveal saturation. If latency rises while throughput stagnates, the DLQ pipeline is likely constrained downstream.

panels.title: Kafka Consumer Internal Latency
panels.description: Shows internal consumer timing (fetch/commit latency) to identify broker interaction delays or consumer coordination overhead that may contribute to lag growth.

panels.title: Mongo Command Latency (p50/p90/p95/p99)
panels.description: Displays MongoDB command latency percentiles for DLQ persistence operations. Tail percentile inflation is an early signal of database contention, slow storage, or query/index inefficiency.

panels.title: Mongo Commands Rate (Success vs Failed)
panels.description: Compares Mongo command success and failure rates by command type. Use this to detect persistence reliability regressions and isolate failing operation classes quickly.

panels.title: HTTP Traffic and Error Rate
panels.description: Shows request throughput and HTTP 4xx/5xx rates for DLQ query APIs. Use this to monitor API demand, client misuse trends, and server-side error spikes.

panels.title: HTTP Latency (p50/p90/p95/p99/mean)
panels.description: Provides HTTP latency distribution across typical, tail, and mean views. Use percentile spread to distinguish isolated outliers from broad degradation affecting most requests.

panels.title: Lag Growth by Topic
panels.description: Shows rate of change for lag max by topic. Positive slope indicates backlog acceleration; near-zero suggests steady-state; negative slope indicates recovery and backlog drain.

panels.title: Persisted Jobs by Type
panels.description: Table of persisted DLQ job counts by job type over the selected period. Useful for understanding dominant failure categories and validating operational remediation priorities.

panels.title: Mongo Throughput by Command and Status
panels.description: Shows Mongo operation throughput split by command and status. Use this to correlate write/read volume with failure states and detect abnormal command mix changes.

panels.title: JVM Memory
panels.description: Tracks heap and non-heap JVM memory for dlq-service. Helps identify memory pressure, long-term growth patterns, and potential GC stress under high DLQ load.

panels.title: Process RSS and CPU
panels.description: Shows process-level resident memory and CPU utilization. Use this to detect host resource bottlenecks that can degrade persistence throughput and API responsiveness.

panels.title: Threads, GC, Uptime
panels.description: Combines thread count, GC pause behavior, and uptime for runtime stability monitoring. Useful to spot thread leaks, pause regressions, and restart instability.

panels.title: Mongo and API Throughput
panels.description: Correlates Mongo command rate with HTTP request rate to show data-plane and control-plane demand together. Helps validate whether API traffic aligns with backend persistence load.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, service, uri) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0))))

panels.title: dlq-service — Top 10 Slowest Endpoints (p99)
panels.description: Top 10 HTTP endpoints ranked by p99 latency. Computed as a point-in-time snapshot over the selected rate interval.

panels.title: dlq-service — Total API Requests (Range)
panels.description: Total HTTP requests across selected applications (excluding actuator) over the dashboard time range.

panels.title: dlq-service — Success Rate %

targets.expr: clamp_max(clamp_min(100 * sum(clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", outcome="SUCCESS", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)) / clamp_min(sum(clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)), 1e-9), 0), 100)

panels.description: Percentage of HTTP requests completing successfully, based on the current rate.

panels.title: dlq-service — HTTP Error Rate %

targets.expr: clamp_max(clamp_min(100 * sum(clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", status=~"4..|5..", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)) / clamp_min(sum(clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)), 1e-9), 0), 100)

panels.description: Percentage of HTTP requests returning 4xx or 5xx status codes.

panels.title: dlq-service — p95 Latency
panels.description: 95th percentile HTTP request latency across all selected applications.

panels.title: dlq-service — p99 Latency
panels.description: 99th percentile HTTP request latency across all selected applications.

panels.title: dlq-service — Request Rate (r/s)
panels.description: Current HTTP request throughput in requests per second.

targets.expr: sum by (service) ((http_server_requests_active_seconds_gcount{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}) or (http_server_requests_active_seconds_active_count{service="dlq-service", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}))

panels.title: dlq-service — HTTP In-Flight Requests — Active Concurrency
panels.description: Number of HTTP requests currently being processed (active / in-flight) per application. Uses Micrometer LongTaskTimer active-count metric with compatibility fallback for both OpenMetrics variants (`http_server_requests_active_seconds_gcount` and `http_server_requests_active_seconds_active_count`). Sustained high values indicate thread-pool saturation or upstream blocking. Sudden spikes coinciding with p99 latency rises confirm concurrency-driven slowdowns.

title: DLQ Service
